events.title: US Military Implements Blockade of Iranian Ports
events.desc: Following failed peace talks with Iran, the U.S. military, through U.S. Central Command, announced it would begin a blockade of all maritime traffic entering and exiting Iranian ports and coastal areas. This action escalates tensions and jeopardizes a fragile two-week ceasefire, raising concerns about fresh retaliation from Tehran and significant strain on regional stability.

events.title: Oil Prices Surge Past $103 Amidst US Blockade Threat
events.desc: Asian stocks fell and oil prices surged past $103 a barrel following the U.S. announcement of a potential blockade of all Iranian ports. The threat of naval action in the Strait of Hormuz injects new turmoil into global financial markets and highlights the potential economic consequences of the escalating conflict.

events.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guards Threaten Response to Military Vessels Near Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: Iran's Revolutionary Guards declared that any military vessels approaching the Strait of Hormuz would be considered a violation of the U.S. ceasefire and would be met with a harsh response. This statement underscores Iran's determination to control its waterways and signals a potential for direct confrontation if the U.S. blockade is enforced.

events.title: Lebanese PM Seeks Israeli Troop Withdrawal Amidst Ongoing Tensions
events.desc: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam is actively working to de-escalate the conflict with Israel, aiming for an Israeli troop withdrawal from southern Lebanon. However, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has indicated that military operations in the region are far from over, highlighting the continued volatility on the Lebanese border.

events.title: Saudi Arabia Summons Iraqi Envoy Over Drone Threats
events.desc: Saudi Arabia summoned Iraq's ambassador to address concerns about drone threats originating from Iraqi territory targeting the Kingdom and other Gulf states. This diplomatic action indicates growing regional unease over the use of proxy forces and the potential for cross-border attacks.

events.title: Large Wave of Israeli Strikes Hits Lebanon Post-Ceasefire Announcement
events.desc: Hours after a U.S.-Iran ceasefire was announced, Israel launched significant strikes across Lebanon, including Beirut's southern suburbs and the Bekaa Valley. These attacks resulted in at least 182 deaths and 890 injuries, marking a severe violation of the perceived ceasefire and drawing strong condemnation from Iran.

events.title: Iran Executes at Least 1,639 People in 2025, Highest Since 1989
events.desc: Human rights organizations report that Iran executed at least 1,639 people in 2025, marking the highest number of executions since 1989. This surge, attributed to increased crackdowns following protests and the ongoing conflict, includes 48 women, indicating a severe human rights crisis within the country.

events.title: Israel Approves New Mossad Chief Amidst Regional Unrest
events.desc: Israel's government has approved the appointment of Roman Gofman as the new head of the Mossad intelligence agency. This decision comes as regional conflicts intensify and highlights Israel's focus on maintaining its intelligence capabilities amidst a complex security landscape.

events.title: US Military Strikes Damage E-3 Sentry and KC-135 Tankers in Saudi Arabia
events.desc: Iranian drones and missiles struck Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, destroying an E-3 Sentry airborne command center and damaging multiple KC-135 tankers. These attacks represent a significant success for Iran's asymmetric counterair campaign, targeting critical U.S. air assets in the region.
events.location: Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia

events.title: US Deploys Mines Over Iranian Village, Causing Fatalities
events.desc: Evidence suggests the U.S. deployed the Gator Scatterable Mine system over the village of Kafari in southern Iran, resulting in several fatalities according to Iranian media. This action marks a significant escalation in direct kinetic engagement within Iranian territory.

events.title: Iranian Government Imposes Internet Blackout Amidst Strikes
events.desc: During coordinated strikes against military installations, Iran's government deliberately dismantled communication infrastructure, causing an internet traffic collapse of 98%. This near-total blackout was imposed on the Iranian populace, contrasting with external strikes aimed at military targets.

events.title: Hungarian Government Passwords Exposed Online
events.desc: Approximately 800 Hungarian government email addresses and passwords have been leaked online, exposing basic security vulnerabilities across 12 of the government's 13 ministries. This data breach raises concerns about the security of sensitive and classified information.

infrastructure.name: Prince Sultan Air Base
infrastructure.note: Royal Saudi Air Force base and key US CENTCOM hub
